conversations.content: Today we studied the Experimental Middle School Mobile Phone and Electronic Device Use Code. If a student is found carrying a mobile phone but not using it for the first time in the teaching area...
Carrying but not playing? The first offense shouldn't be too harsh, right?
then confiscate the phone until dismissal and record their class and name.
Confiscate until dismissal plus record — a reminder. What about using it in class to play games or watch videos?
If a student uses their phone during class to play games or watch videos, then confiscate the phone for one week and have a parent pick it up, and deduct 10 conduct points.
Confiscate for a week plus parent pickup, and deduct 10 conduct points — much heavier! What about photographing others' privacy or distributing inappropriate content?
If a student uses their phone to photograph others' privacy or distribute inappropriate content, then issue a demerit and order deletion of all content.
Privacy photos or inappropriate distribution — demerit plus deletion, legal issues involved! What about repeated offenders who refuse to correct?
If a student violates the rules three or more times and refuses to correct their behavior, then place them on probation and ban them from bringing any smart devices to school.
Three times and no correction — probation plus ban on smart devices, that finally cuts off the temptation!

think: Experimental Middle School phone rules: first-time carry no use → confiscate until dismissal + record; in-class use games/videos → confiscate 1 week + parent pickup + deduct 10 conduct points; photograph privacy/distribute inappropriate → demerit + order deletion; three-plus violations refusing correction → probation + ban any smart devices.
memory: Experimental Middle School Mobile Phone and Electronic Device Use Code: If a student is found carrying a mobile phone but not using it for the first time in the teaching area, then confiscate the phone until dismissal and record their class and name; if a student uses their phone during class to play games or watch videos, then confiscate the phone for one week and have a parent pick it up, and deduct 10 conduct points; if a student uses their phone to photograph others' privacy or distribute inappropriate content, then issue a demerit and order deletion of all content; if a student violates the rules three or more times and refuses to correct their behavior, then place them on probation and ban them from bringing any smart devices to school.
